item.title: No Kings Boston
item.description: First I want to tell everybody who was part of the protest anywhere that I am proud of you. Seven million people in the United States protest the current regime and very little violence none perpetrated by any of the protesters. FP question : I have a legitimate question? What did this protest actually accomplish? It seems like it has been largely ignored by any of the authorities in the White House. They did their typical lanting and raving and pointing fingers, calling people communist, etc. But what will all this effort change? Not trying to be the pessimist here, but a weekend of protest will do nothing. If we look back at history, the discount army Occupied the nation's capital for a very long time. And only then did a little bit of change come from it. If you can help me answer this question in the comments down below, I would greatly appreciate it.
item.dc:creator: SquaresNews

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: My friend @rbeeseart painted these tiny birds!! Just beautiful.
Man Tax: Zayn Malik
“The one thing that is more dangerous than true ignorance is the illusion of understanding.” - A.C. Grayling I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting only on that, also comment about something you DID like. Balance the negativity. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We're also setting up Jack Box game night and movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work and pretty destitute. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, you are the best. You've helped me so much. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me!
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Just a Number
item.description: Edit 2: It's amazing that there are a few shitheads on here that are blaming troops for the politicians wars A little backstory. I signed up in 2008 when the economy failed. There were no trade schools, no job opportunities. Nothing that could save me from the inevitable homelessness. I was going to have. My father was one of those assholes that was kicking me out without any help. I had no family, no safety not to fall on. But there were two wars brought on by Bush. There are a few toxic people down below who really think that it's the fault of people like me who had no options That made the choices that sent us to Afghanistan and Iraq. Often times, I find these people are one of two categories. Most times, people who couldn't muster the courage to serve their community, let alone their country. Often times, that don't even participate in the systems that could fix things, like voting. A lot of tankies, as we call them, the same people who abstain their vote for Kamala Harris because of Joe Biden's handling of Gaza. Or the alternative, the far right looms that everybody hates known as MAGA. That one's pretty self-explanatory. The things that fucked me up the most that I can share are seeing that religious extremists went into places far before we got there and were abusing the children and women because they wanted to have some semblance of a normal life. I remember two little girls that we investigated that were hit with Boric acid by a dude on a motorcycle because they weren't supposed to be going to school. It was my intelligence gathering and scrutinizing that led to us finding the person that did it to sell the operated out of and the people that coerced that sell and to do in horrible things. In the end, in West Afghanistan, we ended up interdicting and stopping people from attacking women. Now, this isn't saying the middle theory hasn't fucked up and done horrible things. That is not what I'm saying. But... At the root of it, just like now, it was a Republican politician that pushed us into the conflicts for their own inflated egos and reasons. I wish I was not 35 years old with a broken body that could have opportunities to live a normal life. I could barely eat normally. I could barely function physically on a semi regular basis. I by no means am special, but do not deserve the ire of the far left who contribute nothing at all, or the far right who are attacking and destroying everything. But hey, continue to push people who are already not doing well and pushing that upper and upper upwards. Good on you for being that type of person. Fp Edit: I appreciate the kind of people in the comments. I have frankly given up hope on ever feeling better or making progress. I don't think that option is on the table for us anymore. The way I'm looking at it will survive as long as I can and then die and be forgotten. They never will care. And you can't go to the VA for help anymore. The current administration has villainized mental health and has using it to target people. Sincerely, a 100% service connected, prominent, and total disabled Army combat vet. For those of you out there that support us, just want to say thank you. If you want to help me out. https://buymeacoffee.com/gridsquares
item.dc:creator: SquaresNews

item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.title: They can try to delete history, but they can't make us forget it (Recently Deleted History: Harvey Milk)
item.description:
Source is Extra History on youtube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCCODtTcd5M1JavPCOr_Uydg https://www.youtube.com/shorts/MeAW4TFLhbY Sad trivia: Harvey Milk's assassination was why the Dead Kennedys did their own version of the song "I Fought the Law" with changed lyrics. Milk and the Mayor of San Francisco, George Moscone, were both killed at the same time by another city supervisor and ex-soldier/ex-cop Dan White. Instead of White being convicted of murder, he was found guilty of the lesser charge of manslaughter due to an argument by his lawyers that White was depressed and of a compromised mental state. One piece of evidence they cited as proof of White's depression was White's change in diet from healthy food to sugary snacks like Twinkies. Original lyrics to I Fought The Law:
And here's the version with changed lyrics by the Dead Kennedys:
And one last bit of trivia about this piece of history: as mentioned above, both Milk and his killer Dan White were members of the City Supervisors. In the wake of the assassination of both Milk and Mayor Moscone, the president of the Board of Supervisors, some lady named Dianne Feinstein, became Mayor of San Francisco and remained mayor until 1988. Just a few years after she ended her time as mayor, she won election to the Senate. And just a reminder: as small a piece of justice as it was to have Milk's name on a ship of the same Navy that he was once drummed out of, even that is in danger of being lost forever because of the fascists and incompetents currently in office.
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer
